849.reason: Error: Should be utils.preRead(1) instead of utils.preRead(2). The condition checks the second token ahead, but it should check the first token to determine if the first element is a comma, which would indicate an empty list or a list starting with a comma.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  Error: Should be utils.preRead(1) instead of utils.preRead(2). The condition checks the second token ahead, but it should check the first token to determine if the first element is a comma, which would indicate an empty list or a list starting with a comma.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect condition: should check if the first element after '{' is not '}' or ';' to decide whether to parse an expression, not look ahead to the second token for a comma. This skips valid single-element initializers like {5}.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect lookahead: after reading "{", the next token is at preRead(1), not preRead(2). This causes wrong parsing of initializer lists]

2304.reason: Error: If preRead(i) returns null (end of tokens), calling .getRow() on null will cause NullPointerException. Also, if the '=' is not found on same line, the loop may attempt to access index out of bounds leading to IndexOutOfBoundsException.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  Error: If preRead(i) returns null (end of tokens), calling .getRow() on null will cause NullPointerException. Also, if the '=' is not found on same line, the loop may attempt to access index out of bounds leading to IndexOutOfBoundsException.

2307.reason: Error: preRead(i+1) may also return null if out of bounds; comparing null to enum is safe (false) but subsequent logic relies on correct return.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  Error: preRead(i+1) may also return null if out of bounds; comparing null to enum is safe (false) but subsequent logic relies on correct return.

153.reason: [This condition incorrectly matches the 'int main(' of MainFuncDef, causing it to be parsed as a FuncDef, leading to a crash or wrong output when the subsequent MainFuncDef analysis tries to read tokens that are already consumed.]

907.reason: [Incorrect condition: same error as in ConstInitVal. Should parse the first Exp/ConstExp if the next token is not '}' or ';', regardless of whether a comma follows later. This causes failure to parse single-element initializers like {a}.]
